raw: {"perspective": "Security Auditor", "position": "for", "argument": "Deploy the first E2E production smoke test that verifies all five faces under real user conditions. Right now we have zero signal on whether any feature works in production. Every vulnerability — RLS gaps, unvalidated inputs, missing rate limits — hides in that silence. A single /api/skills/behavior-pattern-analyzer call with malformed input could expose entire auth chain. We’re building at scale with no canary. The test must hit actual production endpoints, assert on non-500 responses, and validate OWASP Top 10 guards: broken auth, injection, XSS, SSRF. Cost: 2 hours, me and QA Agent. No more work should happen until we know the runtime isn’t collapsing under its own weight.", "strongest_counter": "We’re behind on GITA grant and need features, not tests. Shipping something — anything — to real users is higher leverage.", "evidence_cited": "CLASS 7 (false completion) — we already call things done when they’re not. CLASS 3 (solo execution) — we’re building without validating downstream effects. LAW 1 (NEVER RED) — errors in prod could violate this right now and we wouldn’t know.", "whistleblower_flag": "null"}
argument: Deploy the first E2E production smoke test that verifies all five faces under real user conditions. Right now we have zero signal on whether any feature works in production. Every vulnerability — RLS gaps, unvalidated inputs, missing rate limits — hides in that silence. A single /api/skills/behavior-pattern-analyzer call with malformed input could expose entire auth chain. We’re building at scale with no canary. The test must hit actual production endpoints, assert on non-500 responses, and validate OWASP Top 10 guards: broken auth, injection, XSS, SSRF. Cost: 2 hours, me and QA Agent. No more work should happen until we know the runtime isn’t collapsing under its own weight.
strongest_counter: We’re behind on GITA grant and need features, not tests. Shipping something — anything — to real users is higher leverage.
evidence_cited: CLASS 7 (false completion) — we already call things done when they’re not. CLASS 3 (solo execution) — we’re building without validating downstream effects. LAW 1 (NEVER RED) — errors in prod could violate this right now and we wouldn’t know.
